executed_sql_statements: SELECT name, type, tbl_name, sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type IN ("table","index","trigger") AND (name IN ("prediction_runs","prediction_items","prediction_results","snapshot_schedule","strategy_prediction_replays","draws") OR tbl_name IN ("prediction_runs","prediction_items","prediction_results","snapshot_schedule","strategy_prediction_replays","draws")) ORDER BY type, name
SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count, SUM(CASE WHEN created_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS created_at_nulls, MIN(created_at) AS min_created_at, MAX(created_at) AS max_created_at FROM prediction_runs

SELECT COUNT(*) AS duplicate_group_count FROM (SELECT lottery_type, latest_known_draw, strategy_name, created_at, COUNT(*) AS n FROM prediction_runs GROUP BY lottery_type, latest_known_draw, strategy_name, created_at HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count, SUM(CASE WHEN created_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS created_at_nulls, SUM(CASE WHEN bet_index IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS bet_index_nulls FROM prediction_items
SELECT COUNT(*) AS duplicate_group_count FROM (SELECT run_id, bet_index, COUNT(*) AS n FROM prediction_items GROUP BY run_id, bet_index HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)

SELECT COUNT(*) AS power_item_count, SUM(CASE WHEN pi.special IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS power_special_nulls FROM prediction_items pi JOIN prediction_runs pr ON pr.id=pi.run_id WHERE pr.lottery_type="POWER_LOTTO"
SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count, SUM(CASE WHEN resolved_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS resolved_at_nulls FROM prediction_results

SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count, SUM(CASE WHEN scheduled_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS scheduled_at_nulls, SUM(CASE WHEN target_draw IS NULL OR target_draw="" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS target_draw_missing FROM snapshot_schedule

SELECT COUNT(*) AS row_count, SUM(CASE WHEN generated_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS generated_at_nulls, SUM(CASE WHEN history_cutoff_draw IS NULL OR history_cutoff_draw="" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS history_cutoff_missing, SUM(CASE WHEN predicted_special IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS predicted_special_nulls FROM strategy_prediction_replays

SELECT COUNT(*) AS duplicate_group_count FROM (SELECT lottery_type, target_draw, strategy_id, replay_run_id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM strategy_prediction_replays GROUP BY lottery_type, target_draw, strategy_id, replay_run_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)

current_prediction_flow.behavior: Generates and returns a prediction response without persisting an auditable prospective record.
Reads history through SQLite URI mode=ro, derives a next-draw label by numeric suffix increment, and writes an artifact outside the database.
Validates quick-predict artifacts, opens a read-only preflight connection, then uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE to insert prediction_runs and prediction_items after an explicit legacy apply id.
gaps: No target_draw column on the persisted prediction.
Duplicate check is a preflight search over notes/review_json rather than an atomic semantic unique key.
The apply id is historical and is not a prospective activation mechanism.

current_prediction_flow.behavior: The last tracked implementation created snapshots and inferred the target from latest-known draw plus one, but both modules are deleted from the current tree while ingest.py still imports them.
Populate the retrospective strategy_prediction_replays store and include repair, update, rollback, and reconstructed paths.

current_result_ingestion_flow.behavior: A fetched draw can be inserted into draws, after which refresh and schedule hooks are attempted.

The route imports this resolver, but the module is absent from the current tree. Its last tracked implementation selected the first draw numerically after latest_known_draw, inserted a separate prediction_results row, and changed item status.
Prediction payload and result payload use separate tables, but prospective membership is not frozen before resolution and target identity is not stored on prediction rows.

identity.evidence: prediction_runs has no target_draw, source_version, protocol version, activation version, or immutable identity. target_draw exists only in snapshot_schedule or nested artifact metadata.
identity.remediation: Persist an immutable batch and ticket identity with canonical lottery_type, target_draw, source/version, strategy/version, bet_index, prediction_created_at, activation_version, and a deterministic content hash.

timestamp availability.evidence: created_at fields exist, but are nullable SQLite defaults and are not explicitly defined as prediction_created_at with causal semantics.
timestamp availability.remediation: Require an application-supplied normalized UTC prediction_created_at, validate it before insert, and retain the database insertion time separately.

authoritative draw-close resolution.evidence: No draw_close_at field or official-source-backed resolver exists; only draw dates and inferred weekday schedules were found.
authoritative draw-close resolution.remediation: Implement a versioned, official-source-verified lottery calendar resolver that produces draw_close_at and fails closed for holidays, exceptions, or unknown schedules.

timezone normalization.evidence: SQLite CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, local astimezone output, naive draw dates, and mixed timestamp formats coexist without a single comparison contract.
timezone normalization.remediation: Resolve source schedules in Asia/Taipei, convert both prediction_created_at and draw_close_at to canonical UTC instants, and reject naive or ambiguous input.

database uniqueness.evidence: snapshot_schedule and replay rows have useful unique constraints, but prediction_runs and prediction_items lack a prospective semantic unique key and target_draw is absent.
database uniqueness.remediation: Add an atomic unique constraint over activation, lottery, target draw, source/version, strategy/version, and ticket identity.

application duplicate prevention.evidence: The controlled apply script checks a fingerprint in notes/review_json before writing, but the check and insert are not protected by a matching database unique constraint.
application duplicate prevention.remediation: Use one transaction plus a database unique key; treat uniqueness conflicts as idempotent rejection and never create a second eligible prediction.

immutability.evidence: No append-only trigger or repository boundary prevents UPDATE or DELETE of prediction payloads.
immutability.remediation: Use append-only prospective tables with database triggers rejecting UPDATE and DELETE and a separate immutable amendment event stream.

update/amendment protection.evidence: Existing code includes status updates, metadata updates, replay timestamp backfills, rollback deletes, and broad administrative deletion capability; no post-close payload guard exists.
update/amendment protection.remediation: Forbid payload mutation, represent corrections as new ineligible amendment events, and automatically invalidate affected clusters without replacing originals.

outcome separation.evidence: prediction_results is separate and prediction generation does not require the result table, but membership and target identity are not frozen before outcome resolution; replay rows co-locate prediction and actual payloads.
outcome separation.remediation: Freeze eligibility in the prediction ledger before any outcome join and keep outcome linkage in a separate table keyed to immutable ticket identity.

POWER second zone.evidence: prediction_items.special is nullable, no conditional constraint exists, and the last scheduler implementation stored one shared second-zone value only on the first POWER ticket.
POWER second zone.remediation: Require a prediction-time second-zone value on every eligible POWER ticket, record its source/version, and reject the whole affected cluster when absent.

prospective membership.evidence: No preregistration_version, prospective_protocol_version, activation_version, immutable eligibility decision, or rejection reason is persisted.
prospective membership.remediation: Create a prospective batch/ticket ledger that computes and stores eligibility once at insert, before outcomes, with fail-closed rejection reasons.

draw clustering.evidence: snapshot_schedule and replay rows can group by lottery_type and target_draw, but production prediction rows cannot do so without a non-authoritative derivation from latest_known_draw.
draw clustering.remediation: Persist a canonical target_draw directly on every batch and ticket, validate it against the authoritative schedule, and use lottery_type plus target_draw as the cluster key.

audit trail.evidence: created_at, notes, review_json, hashes, and schedule rows provide fragments of provenance, but there is no immutable event log, actor identity, deployment version, or activation artifact reference.
audit trail.remediation: Record append-only capture events with actor/service identity, source artifact hash, code/deployment commit, schedule version, transaction id, and activation artifact commit.

backfill exclusion.evidence: The historical scheduler explicitly supported RECONSTRUCTED snapshots and replay scripts support backfill and repair; no schema rule prevents those records from being labelled prospective.
backfill exclusion.remediation: Allow eligible membership only for LIVE_PRE_CLOSE capture mode after activation; permanently reject MANUAL, RECONSTRUCTED, BACKFILL, and post-close records.

concurrent-write safety.evidence: The controlled apply path uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE, but its duplicate preflight occurs on a separate read-only connection and no semantic unique constraint closes the race.
concurrent-write safety.remediation: Perform validation and insert under one write transaction with busy timeout, foreign keys, an atomic unique constraint, and deterministic conflict handling.

activation mechanism.evidence: No activation ledger, deployment gate, feature flag, or merged activation artifact exists. The P271G marker remains intentionally unresolved.
activation mechanism.remediation: After implementation and migration verification, merge a separate activation artifact whose merge timestamp is the earliest allowed prospective start and whose deployment checks fail closed.

prediction_identity_assessment.current_key: No prospective key. prediction_items has an id and run_id/bet_index; prediction_runs lacks target_draw and source version.
prediction_identity_assessment.manual_override_risk: High because metadata can be stored in mutable text fields and prediction records have no append-only guard.
prediction_identity_assessment.required_identity: activation_version + lottery_type + target_draw + source_id/source_version + strategy_id/strategy_version + bet_index, with prediction_created_at and content hash retained immutably.

timestamp_assessment.finding: Storage timestamps exist, but they do not prove prediction creation before close and are not consistently timezone-aware.

draw_close_assessment.finding: The repository has date and weekday inference only; it has no authoritative closing instant, exception calendar, or source verification record.

timezone_assessment.finding: No single Asia/Taipei-to-UTC normalization contract is enforced across persisted timestamps and schedule dates.

immutability_assessment.finding: Current stores are mutable and no trigger or repository policy protects original predicted payloads after creation or close.

duplicate_prevention_assessment.finding: Some schedule/replay uniqueness and one application fingerprint guard exist, but no atomic prospective semantic key exists.

outcome_separation_assessment.finding: The prediction tracker schema separates prediction and result tables, but current target/membership binding is insufficient and the live resolver module is absent.

power_second_zone_assessment.finding: The field is nullable and not required per eligible POWER ticket; missing values cannot be repaired prospectively after the fact.

prospective_membership_assessment.preferred_model: Separate append-only prospective batch, ticket, and event ledger rather than repurposing the retrospective replay table.

draw_cluster_assessment.finding: The key is available in schedule/replay stores but not directly on persisted production predictions.

audit_trail_assessment.finding: Existing timestamps and free-form provenance are useful but neither append-only nor complete enough for confirmatory evidence.

backfill_risk_assessment.finding: Reconstructed and manual paths exist historically, and no eligibility field or constraint excludes them from a future population.

concurrency_assessment.finding: BEGIN IMMEDIATE is useful, but the preflight/insert split and missing semantic unique key leave a race.

future_contract: Implementation and additive migration must be separately authorized, merged, deployed, and verified first.
A later activation artifact must name the deployed implementation commit and migration verification evidence.
prospective_prediction_start_at must be no earlier than the activation artifact merge timestamp.
All predictions created before activation remain retrospective.
Activation must fail closed if the capture worker, schedule resolver, database constraints, or immutability guards are not verified.

identified_gaps: No authoritative draw_close_at or exception-aware official schedule resolver.
No target_draw on persisted prediction runs or tickets.
No prospective protocol, preregistration, activation, eligibility, or rejection fields.
No append-only database enforcement or post-close amendment guard.
No atomic prospective semantic unique key.
POWER second-zone value is not required on every eligible ticket.
No immutable prospective audit event log.
No hard exclusion for reconstructed, manual, backfill, or post-close records.
Current ingest hooks reference deleted prediction tracker and snapshot scheduler modules.
No deployed capture worker or activation mechanism.

required_implementation_controls.control: Add separate append-only prospective batch, ticket, and event tables; do not reuse strategy_prediction_replays as the confirmatory population.

Persist canonical target_draw, prediction_created_at_utc, draw_close_at_utc, source/version, strategy/version, bet_index, protocol versions, activation version, eligibility, rejection reason, and content hash.
Add a semantic database unique key and perform capture in one BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction with deterministic conflict handling.
Add database triggers rejecting UPDATE and DELETE on prospective prediction payloads and events.
Implement an official-source-verified, versioned Asia/Taipei draw-close resolver with holiday/exception handling and fail-closed unknown states.
Require prediction_created_at_utc < draw_close_at_utc using timezone-aware instants before insertion.
Require a prediction-time second-zone value on every eligible POWER ticket; never fill it from results or a later fallback.
Freeze prospective eligibility before outcome ingestion and keep outcome linkage in a separate table.
Reject MANUAL, RECONSTRUCTED, BACKFILL, post-close, ambiguous-time, duplicate, and unknown-schedule captures with immutable reasons.
Record source artifact hash, code/deployment commit, actor/service identity, schedule version, activation artifact commit, and transaction id.
Add synthetic temp-database tests for uniqueness races, immutable triggers, timestamp boundaries, POWER enforcement, and backfill exclusion before any production migration.
Use a separate merged activation artifact after deployment verification; do not derive activation from the P271G merge alone.

proposed_next_task_scope.name: SEPARATE_PROSPECTIVE_CAPTURE_IMPLEMENTATION_TASK_PENDING_USER_AUTHORIZATION
proposed_next_task_scope.objective: Implement and test an append-only prospective capture ledger, authoritative draw-close resolver, and fail-closed one-shot capture worker without activating collection.
proposed_next_task_scope.completion_gate: Synthetic/temp-copy tests and deployment design must pass before a separately authorized migration task; activation remains a later task.

proposed_next_allowed_files.purpose: Define additive prospective ledger schema for new databases.
New fail-closed capture service; no activation in the implementation task.
Versioned authoritative schedule and timezone resolver.
Additive migration implementation tested only against synthetic or temporary database copies in the implementation task.
One-shot capture worker with default no-write preflight; live write and scheduling remain unauthorized.
Versioned schedule source metadata and exception declarations, pending manual official verification.
Synthetic contract tests for causality, uniqueness, immutability, POWER, and contamination guards.
Synthetic concurrent writer and idempotency tests.

single_blocker: A separately authorized schema/runtime implementation and verified authoritative draw-close causality layer are required before activation can be considered.
limitations: This audit inspected source, git history, schema metadata, and bounded structural aggregates only.
No official closing-time source was machine-verified; official source status remains MANUAL_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED.
No row-level prediction or result record was selected or exported.
No performance, prize, overlap, M3+, win-rate, or baseline metric was calculated.
The proposed future whitelist is not authorization to modify production code, migrate a database, deploy a worker, or activate collection.
